name: SwiGLU
oneliner: Element-wise product of a Swish-activated gate projection and a value projection — the de facto standard FFN activation in modern LLMs.
explanation: SwiGLU is a gated activation function that replaced ReLU in the feed-forward blocks of modern transformers. It works by having one linear projection produce gate values through the Swish activation, while a second projection produces the values to be gated. The two are multiplied element-wise before a final down-projection. SwiGLU achieves measurably lower perplexity than ReLU at equivalent compute cost. It is used by virtually every major open-weight LLM including Llama, Mistral, Qwen, and DeepSeek.
fundamentals: SwiGLU(x) = (Swish(xW_gate) ⊙ xW_up)W_down. Swish(z) = z·$\sigma$(z). For LLaMA 7B: W_gate [4096, 11008], W_up [4096, 11008], W_down [11008, 4096]. Params: 3$\times$4096$\times$11008 $\approx$ 135M $\approx$ 8$d^2$. In HuggingFace config: hidden_act='silu' (SiLU is the activation within the GLU structure). Code pattern: act_fn(gate_proj(x)) * up_proj(x) then down_proj(...).
